hypothesis: If equivariance-inducing regularization during training (such as equigrad) causes non-equivariant architectures to converge toward the same representational manifold as architecturally equivariant models, then systematically interpolating the strength of this regularization across an ensemble of otherwise identical non-equivariant models will produce a monotonic increase in both representational alignment with equivariant models and downstream out-of-distribution generalization performance, with the alignment gain saturating at a regularization strength that corresponds to the intrinsic dimensionality plateau characteristic of fully equivariant architectures on the same dataset.
